name: Buckswood IMAX and Dune public lens reference profiles
source_policy: Public facts and original Buckswood mappings only. No proprietary/commercial lens profile coefficients copied.

profiles.basis: Panavision H Series spherical lenses used for Dune Part One's 1.43 IMAX sequences on ARRI Alexa LF.
public_facts: H Series is described by Panavision as classic portrait glass with rounded soft roll-off, pleasing skin tone, elevated blacks, and softer contrast.
Dune Part One sources identify H Series as the spherical lens choice for 1.43 IMAX sequences.

profiles.basis: Panavision Ultra Vista 1.6x anamorphic lenses used for Dune Part One's 2.39 sequences.
public_facts: Ultra Vista is listed by Panavision as 1.6x anamorphic, seven focal lengths, typical T2.5, and full-frame 8K coverage.
Panavision describes traditional flares, vertical bias, organic anamorphic character, and depth.

profiles.basis: ARRI Rental Moviecam spherical lenses used on Dune Part Two.
public_facts: ARRI Rental describes Moviecam as 1980s vintage optics rebuilt for large-format, with smooth focus falloff and controlled character.
ASC notes Moviecam was used where resolution and texture were important.

profiles.basis: IronGlass Soviet-era lenses used on Dune Part Two, including publicly named Helios, Jupiter, and Mir examples.
public_facts: ASC names Jupiter-9 85mm, Helios 44-2 58mm, and Mir-1V 37mm as IronGlass lenses ordered for the production.
ARRI/ASC coverage frames the Soviet-era glass as texture-oriented and characterful.

profiles.basis: Clean 50mm large-format face/wide sweet spot inspired by public IMAX 15/70 and Hoytema/Nolan lens discussions.
public_facts: IMAX 15/70 is a much larger horizontal frame format than standard 35mm open gate.
Public Oppenheimer lens coverage highlights 50mm as an IMAX large-format sweet spot.

profiles.basis: Clean 80mm large-format portrait sweet spot inspired by public IMAX 15/70 and Hoytema/Nolan lens discussions.
public_facts: Public Oppenheimer coverage highlights 80mm alongside 50mm as a core large-format face/portrait focal length.
Panavision credits Oppenheimer with System 65 and Sphero 65 optics.

profiles.basis: Reported Atlas Lens Co. custom spherical IMAX lenses for Dune Part Three.
public_facts: Trade coverage reports custom spherical IMAX lenses for Dune Part Three.
Reported design goals include golden single coating, full 15-perf non-vignetting coverage, short close focus, and unique corner character.
